policy: Every legacy Card must have a frozen functional scope, a reproducible AST-generated TSX prototype and a scripted legacy screenshot before production implementation, plus a source-evidenced compatibility checklist before completion. A Card cannot be marked complete from API, title-level inventory or smoke UI coverage alone.

architectureMigrationGuidance.purpose: Cross-task architecture guidance for NeoView migration. Agents must use this section when choosing task boundaries, reporting progress, or changing shared migration infrastructure.

statusModel.before: A single item-level status mixes core implementation, transport, GUI/CLI/TUI wiring and browser evidence, so completed backend work can appear indistinguishable from a fully migrated capability.
statusModel.after: Report progress independently for core, transport, GUI, CLI, TUI and evidence. Keep the existing item status conservative until every required surface and evidence requirement is complete.
statusModel.difference: Progress reports become composable across parallel agents without allowing partial GUI work or missing end-to-end evidence to be mistaken for completion.
agentInstructions: Do not promote an item to complete/migrated from a single surface or API-level test.
In notes and task handoffs, state which of core, transport, GUI, CLI, TUI and evidence changed, and name the remaining surfaces.
Treat generated checklists as views of machine compatibility sources; edit the source JSON and regenerate rather than editing generated Markdown alone.

readerAppBoundary.before: ReaderApp owns session lifecycle/navigation, presentation, runtime configuration, shell control and several separate optimistic persistence queues with generation and rollback handling.
readerAppBoundary.after: Keep ReaderApp as composition only. Isolate session/navigation, presentation, runtime configuration and shell-control state behind focused hooks or stores; reuse one revisioned serialized-mutation primitive for optimistic writes, confirmation, stale-result rejection and rollback.
readerAppBoundary.difference: Concurrent-write and session-switch semantics have one reusable implementation, which reduces duplicated race handling and makes each state domain independently testable.
agentInstructions: Do not add a new ad-hoc Promise queue, generation counter or confirmed-value ref to ReaderApp when the shared mutation primitive can serve the update.
Keep canonical DTO/CAS behavior in the transport or application boundary; UI hooks coordinate state but must not create a parallel persistence contract.
Preserve current cancellation, stale-session rejection and rollback behavior when moving code.

deliveryBoundary.before: Core or HTTP work can be described as migrated while GUI remains wired through provisional flows or lacks browser-level evidence.
deliveryBoundary.after: Deliver non-visual capability changes through the canonical core/application DTO first, then wire GUI, CLI and TUI to that contract and close lifecycle, responsive and end-to-end evidence explicitly.
deliveryBoundary.difference: Parallel surface work converges on one contract instead of accumulating presentation-specific adapters or storage paths.
agentInstructions: Do not introduce GUI-only persistence, DTOs or local storage as a shortcut for a missing shared capability.
When a task is only core/transport complete, record GUI/CLI/TUI/evidence as remaining rather than implying full feature completion.
Prefer extending the existing canonical DTO and service over duplicating behavior per presentation surface.

buildAuditBoundary.before: The chunk audit encodes individual card paths, separation relationships and byte budgets directly in imperative checks.
buildAuditBoundary.after: Move ordinary lazy level, byte budget and separation metadata into the panel registry or a dedicated manifest; let the audit traverse that metadata while retaining a small set of architectural leak checks for entry, sidebar and Node-only modules.
buildAuditBoundary.difference: Adding or moving a Card changes its declared delivery metadata once instead of duplicating architectural facts across registry, audit and migration notes.
agentInstructions: Until a manifest exists, update the registry, audit and compatibility source together when changing a Card's loading boundary.
Do not weaken entry/sidebar isolation or Node-only browser-leak assertions merely to satisfy a new chunk layout.

cliBoundary.before: The NeoView CLI concentrates command inventory, argument parsing, dependency composition, command dispatch and output formatting in one module.
cliBoundary.after: Keep one thin CLI entry router and group handlers by capability: reader session, settings, library, folder, file operations and thumbnail maintenance. Retain shared ReaderService/application contracts.
cliBoundary.difference: Capability changes have smaller conflict surfaces and focused tests without fragmenting the domain/service layer.
agentInstructions: New commands belong to an existing capability module where possible; do not enlarge the top-level router with business logic.
Do not split shared ReaderService/application contracts merely to mirror CLI file boundaries.

implementationOrder: Adopt surface-aware progress reporting and generated status summaries.
Extract the shared revisioned serialized-mutation primitive, then migrate ReaderApp state domains incrementally.
Close GUI wiring and browser evidence against canonical DTOs for capabilities already complete in core/transport.
Introduce declarative build-audit metadata while preserving hard architectural leak checks.
Split CLI handlers by capability only when touching the relevant command family.

requiredDimensions.requirement: Before production React work, generate and review a reproducible svelte/compiler + OXC AST TSX prototype that freezes component/DOM hierarchy, icon imports, classes, conditional/loop structure, control attributes, events and every unsupported source node. A genuinely empty legacy component must instead record source-empty evidence and an explicit replacement design contract; it may not silently bypass this dimension.
Enumerate every user-visible command, mode, data field, batch action and integration from the frozen source.

Before implementation, enumerate every source control, menu item, option value, visible field, shortcut and state with source evidence and map each entry to dedicated acceptance items; summaries are not sufficient.
Freeze layout hierarchy, controls, icon meaning, density, labels and responsive geometry; record intentional XR-token adaptations.
Cover default, hover, focus, selected, active, disabled, loading, empty, partial, error, retry and disposed states.
List defaults, persistence keys, precedence, migration source and reset behavior; target one canonical storage version.
List shortcuts, focus order, semantic roles, keyboard equivalents, IME/input exclusions and screen-reader labels.
Define stable DTOs, identity, pagination/streaming, cancellation, generation, error and stale-result semantics shared by GUI/CLI/TUI.
Define lazy load, open, suspend, resume, close, dispose and failure cleanup; prove background work stops when the Card is inactive.
Set representative corpus, latency, memory, DOM/task/cache bounds and regression budgets; run heavy checks serially on Windows.
Each completed checklist item has stable automated test IDs; GUI behavior includes interaction and screenshot/geometry evidence where relevant.
Any removed, replaced or deliberately changed legacy behavior needs an explicit rationale and replacement contract.

completionGate: A reproducible AST TSX prototype exists for the Card source, records its source hash and unsupported nodes, and has been reviewed before production implementation; genuinely empty source uses a documented source-empty replacement contract.
A terminal-scripted legacy 1920x1080 screenshot exists and the target hierarchy, icons, density and responsive geometry are compared against it.
A source UI inventory exists for the Card and every inventory group maps to source-evidenced acceptance items.
No pending or partial checklist items remain for the Card.
Every item has source evidence and at least one automated test ID.
GUI, CLI and TUI use the same core/application contract where the capability is non-visual.
Visual parity has desktop and constrained-card evidence.
Canonical persistence and legacy import are verified without dual runtime versions.
Lifecycle and performance budgets pass without running heavy jobs concurrently.

capabilityStatus.overall: Derived only: complete when every applicable dimension is complete; pending when every applicable dimension is pending; partial otherwise. not-applicable dimensions do not affect overall status.
capabilityStatus.applicability: GUI/CLI/TUI listed in an item's surfaces are required and cannot be not-applicable. Core and transport applicability follows the canonical application/DTO boundary. Evidence is required for every item.
